title: Ship the box MpiNodes to the Pod (node_lock bump) — blocks RunPod for Head Swap
description:
The Head Swap graph needs MpiBox / MpiBoxCrop / MpiBoxMask, and those nodes exist ONLY in
the local working tree. They are NOT in the pinned MpiNodes commit, so RunPod cannot load
app_head_swap.json at all — ComfyUI rejects the prompt with unknown node types before any
weight is touched. This is a SEPARATE blocker from the LoRA (which is now on R2, MPI-299).

EVIDENCE (2026-07-19):
- dev_configs/node_lock.json pins ComfyUI-MpiNodes at ba9e1569d9f77567907654ae04745580f43f18b9.
- That commit is pushed and IS local HEAD, but `git grep mpi_box ba9e1569` returns NOTHING.
- The box nodes live in UNCOMMITTED edits to img.py + __init__.py in
  engine/ComfyUI_windows_portable/ComfyUI/custom_nodes/ComfyUi-MpiNodes (working tree also
  dirty in README.md, changelog.md, logger.py, .mpi_node_commit).
- app_head_swap.json requires class_types: MpiBox, MpiBoxCrop, MpiBoxMask (plus MpiAnySwitch,
  MpiInt, MpiLoadImageFromPath, MpiMath which DO exist at the pin).

WHY THE POD MISSES THEM: MpiNodes is a VOLUME node (code-only, installRequirements:false), not
baked into the image. The wrapper clones the node_lock commit onto the volume at connect
(cubric-vision-pod/start.sh, README section "Ship a new MpiNodes version"). So the Pod gets
exactly what is PUSHED at the pinned commit — never the local working tree. No image rebuild is
involved; this is a commit-bump path.

STEPS:
1. USER: commit + push the box nodes in the ComfyUi-MpiNodes repo (separate repo:
   github.com/MadPonyInteractive/ComfyUi-MpiNodes). Bump the version + changelog per that
   repo's convention.
2. Bump `commit` for ComfyUI-MpiNodes in Cubric-Vision/dev_configs/node_lock.json to the new SHA.
3. Verify the Pod picks it up on a fresh connect (the wrapper reinstalls a volume node when the
   locked commit changes).
4. THEN MPI-299's RunPod verification can actually run.

DO NOT: bake MpiNodes into the image or rebuild the Pod image for this — it is a volume node
BY DESIGN (MPI-222) precisely because it churns. Bumping the lock is the whole mechanism.

events.note: Split out of MPI-299: the box nodes are not in the pinned MpiNodes commit, so RunPod cannot load the Head Swap graph.

todo -> doing (validating). dev_configs/node_lock.json MpiNodes commit bumped ba9e1569 -> aaa1d2d9fa713decae6843e64f5a81b84f9f443d (repo HEAD = origin/main, v1.2.6). Class-map diff is a pure superset: adds MpiBox, MpiBoxCrop, MpiBoxMask, MpiFromBox; removes nothing, so no existing graph breaks. Remaining proof: confirm the Pod wrapper reinstalls the new volume-node commit on fresh connect and app_head_swap.json stops rejecting unknown node types. Tracked in MPI-324 Phase 0.
